items.final_wording: Defines the Dubai telehealth population and diabetes, antibiotic, estrogen, pregnancy, immunosuppression, medication, and device risk contexts.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Vaginal Candidiasis – 10
source.population: Patients with complaints compatible with vaginal candidiasis.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed healthcare facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for candidiasis-compatible vaginal-discharge documentation in Dubai telehealth; partial for bacterial vaginosis, STI, cervical, pelvic, postmenopausal, pregnancy, and other discharge causes.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the Dubai telehealth population and diabetes, antibiotic, estrogen, pregnancy, immunosuppression, medication, and device risk contexts.

items.rationale: Supports direct Dubai telehealth risk and scope context for possible vulvovaginal candidiasis.

items.final_wording: Specifies discharge character, itching, soreness, dyspareunia, dysuria, onset, duration, recurrence, menstrual and abnormal bleeding, pregnancy, medical, medication, and sexual history.

items.rationale: Supports entered discharge, itching and associated history.

items.final_wording: Lists lower abdominal pain with fever, foul-smelling or blood-stained discharge, postcoital bleeding, intermenstrual bleeding, and recent-partner context.

items.final_wording: Defines when microscopy or culture is not routine, when targeted culture is appropriate, and when pregnancy, systemic illness, atypical symptoms, recurrence, bleeding, or lower abdominal pain require face-to-face or specialist review.
